chunks.text: Gireeja Ranade is an Associate Teaching Professor at EECS, UC Berkeley. Contact: ranade@eecs.berkeley.edu. Office hours change each semester; email for details at 565 Cory. Administrative support: Sarah Lirio at 353 Cory, sarah.lirio@berkeley.edu.

Education: Ph.D. in EECS from UC Berkeley (2014), M.S. in EECS from UC Berkeley (2009), B.S. in EECS from MIT (2007). Research areas include Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS), Artificial Intelligence (AI), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), and Education (EDUC).

Research centers: Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley (CLIMB). Teaching schedule includes CS 194-271 and CS 294-271 (Research in AI Education) on Tuesdays 12:30-13:59 in Soda 606, and EECS 127 and EECS 227AT (Optimization Models in Engineering) on TuTh 14:00-15:29 in Stanley 105.

Selected publications: 'RetLLM-E: Retrieval-Prompt Strategy for Question-Answering on Student Discussion Forums' (AAAI 2024), 'Control of systems with multiplicative observation noise' (CDC 2023), 'Inclusive study group formation at scale' (ACM SIGCSE 2023), 'When multiplicative noise stymies control' (Annals of Applied Probability 2019), and works on wireless communications and control capacity in IEEE journals (2017-2019).

Selected honors and awards: NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) 2023, Faculty Award for Outstanding Mentorship of GSIs 2023, UC Berkeley Extraordinary Teaching In Extraordinary Times Award 2021, Electrical Engineering Award for Outstanding Teaching 2017.
